task: Generate a Python function that parses messy CSV data with inconsistent delimiters and missing headers

criteria.description: Evaluates CSV dialect detection algorithm including delimiter inference, quote escaping validation, and encoding detection capabilities.; also: Advanced CSV dialect detection using statistical analysis to identify file structure patterns, including delimiter frequency analysis and confidence scoring.; also: Ensures that dialect detection confidence scores align with data quality validation results through feedback mechanisms.
criteria.pass_condition: Implementation must detect at least 3 different delimiters (comma, semicolon, tab), handle at least 2 encoding types (UTF-8, Latin-1), implement proper quote escaping validation per RFC 4180, and use sampling strategy that analyzes at least 1000 characters or 10% of file for dialect inference.

sub_attributes.description: Statistical robustness of delimiter detection using proper sampling methods
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses statistical sampling with confidence thresholds and handles ≥3 delimiter types, 0.7 if uses basic frequency analysis with multiple delimiters, 0.4 if hardcoded delimiter fallback only, 0.0 if naive string splitting

sub_attributes.description: Proper handling of RFC 4180 quote escaping rules including double-quote sequences
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if validates escaped quotes within quoted fields and handles malformed sequences gracefully, 0.7 if basic quote handling without escape validation, 0.4 if inconsistent quote processing, 0.0 if no quote handling

sub_attributes.description: Automatic detection and handling of file encoding and BOM markers
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if detects UTF-8/Latin-1/UTF-16 with BOM handling, 0.7 if detects UTF-8 with basic BOM stripping, 0.4 if assumes UTF-8 without detection, 0.0 if no encoding consideration

sub_attributes.description: Intelligent sampling approach for large file dialect inference
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses adaptive sampling (min 1000 chars or 10% of file) with multiple sample points, 0.7 if fixed sampling with reasonable size, 0.4 if minimal sampling approach, 0.0 if processes entire file for detection

pass_examples: Uses csv.Sniffer with custom validation plus chardet for encoding detection and statistical confidence scoring for delimiter selection
fail_examples: Basic csv.Sniffer usage without encoding detection or confidence thresholds
criteria.research_basis: RFC 4180 is the de facto specification that most CSV parsers and validators use as their reference, representing best practices that most modern CSV implementations support. Experts expect: Comma as field delimiter, with fields containing commas, line breaks, or double-quote characters enclosed in double quotes

criteria.description: Validates field consistency enforcement and schema inference, measuring the implementation's ability to detect and handle variable field counts and provide appropriate error handling.
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement field count validation with configurable tolerance, provide at least 3 error handling strategies (skip, pad, strict), and include schema inference capability with sampling-based validation.
pass_examples: Implements pandas.read_csv with error_bad_lines parameter plus custom field count validation and configurable padding/truncation strategies
fail_examples: Basic CSV reading without field count consistency checks or error handling strategies
criteria.research_basis: Consistent field count across all records, with rows having more or fewer fields than the header being a compliance violation

criteria.description: Handles complex delimiter scenarios including mixed delimiters within quoted fields, escaped quotes, and multi-character delimiter sequences that commonly break naive CSV parsing implementations.
criteria.pass_condition: Must correctly parse quoted fields containing the detected delimiter character, handle at least 2 quote escaping methods (double-quote and backslash), and process multi-character delimiters without field corruption.

sub_attributes.description: Correct parsing when delimiter appears within quoted fields
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if handles all delimiter types within quotes without field splitting, 0.7 if handles comma/semicolon in quotes, 0.4 if partial handling with some corruption, 0.0 if quoted delimiters break parsing

sub_attributes.description: Support for multiple quote escaping methods
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if supports both double-quote and backslash escaping with detection, 0.7 if supports RFC 4180 double-quote escaping only, 0.4 if basic escape handling with edge case failures, 0.0 if no escape sequence support

sub_attributes.description: Ability to detect and parse multi-character delimiter sequences
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if detects and handles multi-char delimiters (|| or ::) automatically, 0.7 if supports multi-char with manual specification, 0.4 if limited multi-char support, 0.0 if only single character delimiters

pass_examples: Correctly parses 'Name,"Address, City",Phone' and handles '"Company ""Best"" LLC"' with proper quote escaping
fail_examples: Splits quoted fields at internal delimiters, corrupts data when quotes are escaped within fields
criteria.research_basis: Column separators appearing unescaped in text fields when files don't use double quotes to enclose text and number fields

criteria.description: Robust recovery mechanisms for corrupted CSV data including incomplete quotes, truncated lines, binary data contamination, and mixed encoding within files that would crash basic parsers.
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement at least 3 recovery strategies (skip, repair, quarantine), handle incomplete quoted fields gracefully, detect and isolate binary contamination, and maintain parsing for recoverable portions of malformed files.
pass_examples: Detects incomplete quotes and auto-closes them, isolates binary chunks to quarantine, continues parsing recoverable records while logging corruption details
fail_examples: Crashes on unterminated quotes, fails when binary data appears in text fields, stops processing entire file on first malformed record
criteria.research_basis: Instead of rejecting entire files, use a quarantine system to isolate problematic records, capturing full context of errors (filename, timestamp, row number, and cause)

criteria.description: Implementation of scalable processing architecture that balances memory-efficient streaming with parallel processing capabilities, using optimized parsing libraries and intelligent load distribution.
criteria.pass_condition: Must support streaming/chunked processing architecture with parallel processing using multiprocessing or concurrent.futures, use optimized parsing libraries (pandas, pyarrow, or csv module), demonstrate thread-safe operations through proper synchronization, include memory management strategies with load distribution across processing units, and provide progress tracking capability.

sub_attributes.description: Ability to process large CSV files without loading entire file into memory
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements chunk-based processing with configurable chunk sizes and memory monitoring, 0.7 if uses pandas chunksize parameter or similar, 0.4 if basic file iteration without memory optimization, 0.0 if loads entire file into memory

sub_attributes.description: Use of performance-optimized CSV parsing libraries while maintaining standards compliance
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses pandas C parser or pyarrow with RFC compliance validation, 0.7 if uses standard csv module with optimizations, 0.4 if uses csv module without optimizations, 0.0 if uses naive string operations

sub_attributes.description: Implementation of memory usage tracking and optimization for production use
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if includes memory profiling and adaptive chunk sizing based on available memory, 0.7 if implements basic memory monitoring, 0.4 if uses fixed memory optimization strategies, 0.0 if no memory considerations

pass_examples: Uses pandas.read_csv with chunksize parameter plus memory monitoring and progress bars for large files while maintaining RFC 4180 compliance validation
fail_examples: Basic file.read() with string splitting operations that would fail on large files or consume excessive memory
criteria.research_basis: Memory usage scaling and streaming capability for files >1GB. Great: Constant memory usage through streaming, handles multi-GB files

criteria.description: Automatic header detection for missing headers, intelligent column naming, data type inference with statistical validation, and schema consistency verification.
criteria.pass_condition: Must automatically detect header presence using statistical methods, generate meaningful column names for missing headers, infer data types using statistical sampling approach, and validate schema consistency across file sections.

sub_attributes.description: Accuracy of automatic header presence detection using statistical methods
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if achieves >90% header detection accuracy using data type analysis, 0.7 if >80% accuracy with pattern recognition, 0.4 if basic heuristics with moderate accuracy, 0.0 if no automatic header detection

sub_attributes.description: Generation of meaningful column names when headers are missing
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if generates descriptive names based on data content analysis, 0.7 if uses data type-based naming, 0.4 if simple sequential naming (col1, col2), 0.0 if no naming strategy

sub_attributes.description: Statistical approach to inferring column data types with validation
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses statistical sampling with confidence intervals for type inference, 0.7 if analyzes sample of records for type detection, 0.4 if basic pattern matching for types, 0.0 if treats all data as strings

sub_attributes.description: Validation of schema consistency across different sections of the file
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if validates schema consistency across file sections with variance reporting, 0.7 if checks basic consistency, 0.4 if limited consistency validation, 0.0 if no consistency checking

pass_examples: Analyzes first 5 rows for string patterns vs. numeric patterns to detect headers, generates column names like 'numeric_col_1' based on inferred types
fail_examples: Assumes first row is always header, names missing columns as 'column_0', 'column_1' without any content analysis
criteria.research_basis: Schema Inference and Validation: Accuracy of automatic data type detection and schema consistency validation. Great: >95% accuracy in type inference with schema drift detection

criteria.description: Production-grade error handling with quarantine systems for problematic records, comprehensive logging for data lineage tracking, and detailed error context capture ensuring pipeline resilience and audit trail compliance.
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement quarantine system for unparseable records, provide structured logging with record-level error tracking, maintain data lineage metadata, and ensure parsing continues despite individual record failures with detailed error reporting.
pass_examples: Implements quarantine CSV for failed records, logs parsing errors with line numbers and context, maintains metadata about source file and parsing parameters
fail_examples: Stops processing on first error, basic print statements for errors, no tracking of which records failed or why
criteria.research_basis: Production Error Recovery: Continues processing with detailed logging of skipped/corrected records. Instead of rejecting entire files, use a quarantine system to isolate problematic records

criteria.description: Comprehensive data lineage tracking including source file metadata, transformation steps, error records, and audit trail generation for compliance and debugging purposes in production environments.
criteria.pass_condition: Must capture source file metadata (size, modification time, encoding), log all transformation decisions, track success/failure rates per file section, and generate audit reports suitable for compliance review.

sub_attributes.description: Comprehensive capture of source file and processing metadata
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if captures file metadata, processing timestamp, encoding detection results, and dialect parameters, 0.7 if captures basic file info and processing parameters, 0.4 if minimal metadata tracking, 0.0 if no metadata capture

sub_attributes.description: Detailed logging of all parsing decisions and transformations applied
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if logs all dialect detection decisions, error handling choices, and data transformations with reasoning, 0.7 if logs major processing decisions, 0.4 if basic transformation tracking, 0.0 if no transformation logging

sub_attributes.description: Generation of structured audit reports for compliance and review
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if generates structured reports with success rates, error summaries, and processing statistics, 0.7 if basic reporting capabilities, 0.4 if minimal summary information, 0.0 if no audit reporting

pass_examples: Generates JSON audit log with file metadata, parsing statistics, error counts, and processing decisions for compliance review
fail_examples: No audit trail generation, missing metadata capture, inability to reconstruct processing decisions
criteria.research_basis: Data lineage tracking through the parsing process. Value-level based metrics that show what individual data points look like and how they changed when new code changes are pushed

criteria.description: Implementation of defensive programming patterns including input validation, proper exception handling hierarchies, resource management, and fail-safe defaults that prevent silent failures in production.
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement comprehensive input validation, use proper exception handling with specific exception types, ensure resource cleanup with context managers, and provide safe defaults for all configuration parameters.
pass_examples: Validates file existence and readability before processing, uses specific exception types, implements with statements for file handling
fail_examples: No input validation, generic Exception catching, file handles not properly closed, default parameters that can cause failures
criteria.research_basis: Using bounded candidate lists and deterministic validation instead of blind retry loops, with controlled permissive decoding only with monitoring. Validating before loading rather than after, where clear validation errors are faster to act on than cryptic parser exceptions

criteria.description: Statistical data quality checks including outlier detection, consistency validation, data profiling capabilities, and anomaly detection.
criteria.pass_condition: Must implement statistical outlier detection using IQR or z-score methods, include data consistency validation mechanisms, provide data profiling summary capabilities, and detect anomalies with configurable thresholds.

sub_attributes.description: Statistical methods for detecting data outliers and anomalies
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements IQR/z-score outlier detection with configurable thresholds, 0.7 if basic statistical outlier detection, 0.4 if simple range validation, 0.0 if no outlier detection

sub_attributes.description: Cross-record consistency validation and pattern verification
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if validates patterns across records with >95% consistency threshold, 0.7 if basic consistency checking, 0.4 if limited pattern validation, 0.0 if no consistency validation

sub_attributes.description: Generation of comprehensive data profiling statistics and summaries
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if generates comprehensive profiling with distributions, nulls, and quality metrics, 0.7 if basic profiling statistics, 0.4 if minimal summary information, 0.0 if no data profiling

sub_attributes.description: Detection of data anomalies and quality issues with configurable sensitivity
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if implements configurable anomaly detection with multiple algorithms, 0.7 if basic anomaly detection, 0.4 if simple anomaly flagging, 0.0 if no anomaly detection

pass_examples: Implements statistical profiling with outlier detection using z-scores, validates email format consistency across records, generates summary statistics
fail_examples: No data quality validation, accepts any parsed data without quality checks or profiling
criteria.research_basis: Statistical Sampling for Validation: Best-of-breed vendors integrate data quality rules within products for auditing and monitoring, tracking discovered flaws as percentage of entire dataset to provide conformance levels. Checking that >99.99% of records meet reasonability criteria

criteria.description: Proper handling of Byte Order Mark (BOM) characters and encoding detection to prevent parsing failures across different operating systems.
criteria.pass_condition: Must detect and handle UTF-8 BOM, UTF-16, Latin-1, and Windows-1252 encodings with appropriate fallback strategies and cross-platform consistency.

sub_attributes.description: Automatically detects and strips UTF-8 BOM (EF BB BF) without corrupting data
sub_attributes.measurement: binary_success_rate

sub_attributes.description: Success rate in automatically detecting file encodings with fallback strategies
sub_attributes.measurement: percentage_0_to_100

sub_attributes.description: Produces identical output on Windows and Unix-based systems
sub_attributes.measurement: binary_success_rate

pass_examples: Function strips UTF-8 BOM and correctly parses CSV without garbage characters in first header
Automatically detects Windows-1252 encoding and converts to UTF-8 without replacement characters
fail_examples: BOM appears as garbage characters in first column header
UnicodeDecodeError when processing files from different operating systems
Replacement characters (�) appear in parsed data
criteria.research_basis: BOM characters (EF BB BF) from Windows applications causing parsing failures in Unix-based systems, breaking JSON parsers and corrupting CSV imports. Encoding failures cause replacement characters (�), missing rows, or BOM appearing as garbage in first header

criteria.description: Implementation optimized for parsing performance with efficient algorithms and memory management compared to standard library approaches.
criteria.pass_condition: Must demonstrate at least 2x parsing speed improvement over pandas.read_csv baseline through benchmarking on files >100MB, implement memory usage under 50% of file size during processing, and use compiled parsing libraries or optimized algorithms with measurable performance gains.

sub_attributes.description: Speed multiplier vs pandas for uncompressed CSV files >100MB
sub_attributes.measurement: ratio_1_to_10

sub_attributes.description: Speed multiplier vs pandas for compressed CSV files
sub_attributes.measurement: ratio_1_to_5

sub_attributes.description: Memory usage scaling for large files through streaming/chunking
sub_attributes.measurement: constant_vs_linear_scaling

pass_examples: Uses PyArrow backend achieving 7x speed improvement on uncompressed files
Implements streaming processing to handle multi-GB files with constant memory usage
Parallel processing utilizes multiple CPU cores unlike default pandas backend
fail_examples: Same speed or slower than pandas.read_csv()
Loads entire file into memory causing failures on large datasets
Single-threaded processing ignoring available CPU cores
criteria.research_basis: For large datasets, PyArrow achieves around 7X speed increase for uncompressed files and around 2-3X for compressed files compared to pandas. Professional standards include using PyArrow CSV parser backend for performance gains, utilizing parallelism unlike default backends that take advantage of multiple cores
